title_guess: Dinas, Welshpool railway, Harlech & Barmouth with the Portals

pages.text:
John & Charles were too tired for another walk, so we rested in the van while the others walked around Robin Hood's Butts. It was warmer
	After tea we set off home, after the children had watched some model aeroplane flying.

May 30th 1990.  We have set off for __Dinas__ with Matt Portal, Claire 7 & Peter 4.  Jane is in Korea.
	On the way, Simon noticed the Welshpool steam railway. So at the next station, Eve & all the children went by train for 20 minutes to the terminus. Matt & Simon & Charles overtook them in the car & van. It was lovely & leafy & green after the hot van. We crossed a stream &

rode along by it. The train was smokey & smelly & had wooden seats.
	When we all met up we had a picnic lunch & drove on to Dinas.
	The next day we left on a tricky walk from the campsite – rocky – every footstep counted. Claire found it rather hard work. We had elevenses on the walk. Just when we had a clear view of the main road, we came to the top of a rise of rocks … and there was a deep, tree-filled gorge between us & the road. So we had to go back across a barbed-wire fence & find a way around it. Simon raced home with Charles on the pack to get lunch ready (leaving me to piggyback John) only to find the soup still frozen.

Catering for 10 per meal is quite a challenge, hampered by "Eve I don't like tomatoes/porkpies/chicken/dressing" etc. from Peter + Claire. However, they seem to be surviving on bread + cheese.
	On Friday we went to Harlech Castle. The rain bucketed down + we forgot to get out the gumboots. Feeling very cold we toured the Castle + Shops + descended yet again on the fish + chip shop "Eve, I like chips, but I don't like fish....."
	On Friday afternoon we gathered in "Matt's tent". Fortunately we can all just fit in the sitting-room part of it, around the grey table. Matt + Claire + Peter have the sleeping

pages.sketch_desc: Two hand-drawn sketches of tents, not a route map. Top sketch shows a dome tent labelled 'East Wind' with annotation 'Sleeps 2 boys, but could sleep 3.' Bottom sketch shows a larger frame/ridge tent labelled 'Cabanon' with annotations 'Sleeps 6 in back compartments.' and 'Room for grey table in sitting area'. The bottom sketch includes a small label that appears to read 'CABANON' on the tent bag/carry case at the base.
pages.text:
areas.
	So we now have 2 tents.

East Wind.

				Sleeps 2 boys,
				but could sleep 3.

[hand-drawn sketch of dome tent labelled "East Wind"]

Cabanon

				Sleeps 6 in
				back compartments.

				Room for grey
				table in
				sitting area

[hand-drawn sketch of frame tent labelled "Cabanon"]

Matt + the children are very good company - Matt smokes delicious-smelling cigars. There are several games of cricket with other children on the campsite too.
    On Saturday we go to the arts crafts village which has a very good acquarium, lobsters, octopi, trout, crabs. There is also a museum with sights + sounds of yesteryear - a blacksmiths forge, a carpenter /coffin-maker / stores. The RAF was stationed at Barmouth in WWII, so there's an aircraft museum too , + a children's playground.
    We returned to the van for lunch - the Portals perched on the back bumper with the doors hinged all the way back for the first time.

The Portals have left this afternoon, + Eve has been napping in the van with John + Charles while Simon takes Thomas, William + Peter on a very long walk - 2pm - 6 pm ? To be followed by a barbque?
    As well as the tent, we have added other supplies to the camping van this summer - a cutlery drainer, changing the double sleeping bag into 2 duvet-style quilts + fitted bottom sheet. Simon has enlarged the entrance to some of the cupboards + built a box to hold maps on his door. We now have 3 small grey bags for dirty clothes. Charles has a specially notified cardboard box to travel in so that he can't climb about.

June 3rd.

    We pack up at Dinas and set off for home.  We go north & have lunch at Celyn reservoir.
    On the way we pass some good views of Harlech Castle.  We also notice a good campsite on a promontary above Harlech - Merthyr Campsite.  A working farm with cows, __beautiful__ views across to Port Merion, very windy.  2½ mile walk down to the sea.  We may try it next year.
    In the afternoon we visit the highest waterfall in Wales, before reaching home.
